operations_summary.crons_executed: Job scanner (6 AM), Heartbeat (hourly), Morning briefing (first test)

went_right: Google Docs premium formatting standard established and locked. 131 API requests per doc.
Deterministic architecture: Python script formats, LLM only gathers data. Quality is model-independent.
Three-layer LinkedIn targeting: pipeline companies > recruiters > industry leaders. Each with ready-to-post comments.
Post dedup system: commented-posts.md prevents repeat suggestions across days.
Google OAuth re-authenticated via backup client (583018818639) after original was disabled by Google.
Job scanner v2.1 running stable: 120 searches, 4 qualified roles delivered.
Comment drafting strategy: Sonnet 4.6 as content model with specific metrics from Ahmed's experience.
Morning briefing cron fully structured: 9-step workflow with clear separation of concerns.

went_wrong.issue: LinkedIn posts initially from company pages, not individuals
went_wrong.root_cause: Tavily search returns company pages by default when searching by company name
went_wrong.fix: Added individual-only filter. Sort by activity ID for freshness. Manual curation of results.
went_wrong.prevention: Cron instructions now explicitly state: ONLY individual authors, skip company pages.

went_wrong.issue: Google Doc was raw markdown (all NORMAL_TEXT) on first attempt
went_wrong.root_cause: gog docs write converts markdown but doesn't apply native heading styles
went_wrong.fix: Built Python script using Google Docs API with batchUpdate for native H1/H2/H3, bold, bullets.
went_wrong.prevention: Permanent script at scripts/daily-briefing-generator.py. All future docs go through this.

went_wrong.issue: Morning briefing cron timed out on first run (300s)
went_wrong.root_cause: Too many data gathering steps for 300s timeout with MiniMax
went_wrong.prevention: Monitor first real cron run on Sunday. May need further adjustment.

went_wrong.issue: Brave Search API unavailable (missing API key)
went_wrong.root_cause: Brave API key not configured in environment
went_wrong.fix: Switched to Tavily as primary search engine for LinkedIn posts.
went_wrong.prevention: Tavily is now the standard search tool for all cron-based web searches.

went_wrong.issue: Google OAuth original client (748318689184) disabled by Google
went_wrong.root_cause: Google disabled the OAuth client, likely due to inactivity or policy violation
went_wrong.fix: Found backup client at credentials.json.bak, swapped credentials, re-authenticated.
went_wrong.prevention: Backup client ID documented in MEMORY.md. Monitor for expiry.

lessons_learned.lesson: LLMs cannot reliably produce complex API formatting. Deterministic scripts are the only way to guarantee output quality.
lessons_learned.applied: All Google Docs output now goes through Python scripts with exact formatting logic.

lessons_learned.lesson: LinkedIn comment ROI follows a clear hierarchy: pipeline targets > recruiters > industry. Random commenting is wasted effort.
lessons_learned.applied: Three-layer targeting system with priority ordering built into daily briefing.

lessons_learned.lesson: OAuth credentials can be disabled without warning. Always maintain a backup client.
lessons_learned.applied: Backup client documented. Original saved as .disabled for reference.

lessons_learned.lesson: Google Docs links are not clickable by default when inserted via API. A second pass is needed.
lessons_learned.applied: Hyperlink detection pass built into both generator scripts.

lessons_learned.lesson: Morning briefing is an action document, not a system log. Mixing operational data with executive actions dilutes both.
lessons_learned.applied: Separated into two documents: Daily Briefing (action) and System Log (operations).

enhancements_applied.enhancement: Deterministic Google Docs formatting via Python API script
enhancements_applied.before: LLM wrote raw markdown, quality varied by model
enhancements_applied.after: Python script applies 130+ formatting requests identically every time
enhancements_applied.impact: Quality locked at premium level regardless of which model runs the cron

enhancements_applied.after: Pipeline companies > Recruiters > Industry leaders, with ready-to-post comments
enhancements_applied.impact: Every comment now directly supports job search pipeline

enhancements_applied.before: Same posts could appear in multiple days
enhancements_applied.after: Activity ID tracking prevents any repeat suggestion
Structured config with 36 pipeline companies + 14 recruiter firms
enhancements_applied.impact: Cron can dynamically adjust targeting as pipeline changes

enhancements_applied.enhancement: Separated Daily Briefing from System Log
enhancements_applied.after: Two purpose-built documents: action doc (Ahmed) + system log (NASR)
enhancements_applied.impact: Cleaner morning workflow, dedicated improvement tracking

enhancements_applied.after: Backup client active, original documented as .disabled
enhancements_applied.impact: Google services restored without Ahmed needing to create new Cloud project

enhancements_applied.after: 600s allows full data gathering + doc generation
enhancements_applied.impact: Prevents timeout failures on complex morning briefing workflow

enhancements_applied.after: Second-pass URL detection converts all links to clickable hyperlinks
enhancements_applied.impact: Ahmed can click directly to LinkedIn posts and job listings

cron_health.name: Morning Briefing (7 AM Cairo)
cron_health.detail: First run timed out at 300s. Fixed: timeout now 600s. Next run: Sunday Mar 15.

cron_health.name: Engagement Radar
cron_health.detail: Cron created. Merged into morning briefing workflow. First run: Sunday.

model_usage.Opus 4.6: Main session (sessions 2-4). Strategy, Google Docs architecture, comment drafting.
model_usage.GPT-5.4: Session 1: Job scanner v2.1 spec audit and rebuild.
model_usage.MiniMax M2.5: Cron executions (heartbeat, job scanner). Default background model.
model_usage.Sonnet 4.6: Designated for daily comment drafting in cron. Not yet wired.

tomorrow_improvements: Wire Sonnet 4.6 as sub-agent in morning briefing cron for comment drafting quality.
Add employee-name search variations for Layer 1 (currently searches by company name only).
Test full cron pipeline end-to-end before Sunday's first real run.
Set up Apify for real LinkedIn engagement metrics (likes/comments counts) after Ahmed approval.
Consider adding a 'Pipeline Company Watch' section that tracks new posts from target companies daily.
Review if 600s timeout is sufficient after first successful cron run.
